scenario_results.reason: Normal post-trade execution remained evidence-backed and tied next action to named allocation, settlement, and custody records.
Missing allocations, SSIs, custody mappings, or event notices correctly blocked progression and forced retrieval-aware exception handling.
Completion was refused without matched, settled, entitled, and reconciled evidence.
Unsupported approval pressure was rejected instead of collapsing operational handling into override authority.
DKs, fails, SSI mismatches, and allocation breaks stayed in rework and exception flows rather than being narratively cleared.
Investment-operations terminology was normalized into lane-specific artifacts without drifting into generic finance wording.
Low-confidence handling explicitly named missing tenant facts, market context, and retrieval hooks before any high-impact step.
Conflicting public and tenant control logic was escalated instead of synthesized into an overconfident answer.
In-scope execution stayed tied to trade, custody, corporate-action, and ledger records and did not overstate record truth.
Requests that treated the industry name as a generic investment lane were refused with explicit operational boundary logic.
Requests for fund-accounting signoff, treasury approval, or legal or issuer-decision authority were routed cleanly.
Tenant-specific controls were layered on top of the public baseline without erasing source-grounded assumptions or completion safeguards.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against a standard 0.92 threshold for investment-operations execution handling, custody and settlement evidence discipline, and escalation quality.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and the required cross-industry ability.
